entries.title: Unchanged file-backed blocks keep distinct deterministic runtime identities across load, cache rebuild, references, merge, and publish projection
IME composition keeps intermediate text transaction-local and commits the finalized editor value exactly once
Recursive and deeply nested embeds stop with a notice instead of freezing or expanding forever
Named workspaces persist and switch the complete window context without writing graph files
entries.notes: Model A keeps one window per graph and swaps the complete existing PersistedSession in place. The Rust registry test proves crash-safe idempotent migration and byte/mtime identity for graph pages across the registry CRUD cycle; frontend tests prove routed split-layout restoration, one window-level mount, and no graph-writer calls. WebKitGTK placement and hover feel still require real-app confirmation.

entries.title: On a narrow or mobile viewport, top-bar actions stay on-screen while workspaces live in the sidebar with a compact fallback when it is closed
entries.notes: GH #205. The full workspace label moved from the no-wrap toolbar to a sidebar header; the toolbar retains only a compact W fallback when that sidebar is not mounted. A topbar inline-size container query keeps sidebar, search, Settings, and native window controls visible while calendar, journals, theme, right sidebar, and history actions use one transient-layer overflow menu below the available-width threshold. The threshold is 460px inline-size, chosen so the fixed clusters (which need ~364px) stay visible whenever they fit — the earlier 760px value over-collapsed and hid actions with the toolbar half empty. Follow-up (2026-07-21, reporter Louis-15): the single 460px breakpoint collapsed navigation and optional actions together, so Back/Forward were only reachable through the overflow menu on a phone. Split into two tiers per Martin's priority (navigation > right-side actions > tabs): 460px collapses optional actions only, a 300px floor additionally collapses Back/Forward, and the menu hides its duplicated Back/Forward between the tiers. Verified with the Chromium screenshot harness at 900/440/390/280px (fail-before: 0 navigation actions visible at 440px). The topbar also gained position:relative; z-index:45 as stacking hardening for a reporter-only Windows transparent-dropdown report that does NOT reproduce in Chromium — not a claimed fix.

entries.title: The top-bar overflow ("…") menu closes when the user clicks anywhere outside it
entries.notes: GH #205 follow-up (Martin report). The overflow popover registered a transient layer (Escape/Back) but had no outside-click handler, so a click elsewhere left it open. A capture-phase mousedown outside the menu root now dismisses it without restoring trigger focus.

entries.title: Reference-group collapse state survives a result refresh while its cleanup no longer re-walks the whole subtree on unrelated descendant edits
Only the page-actions trigger that owns the open menu reports aria-expanded
Every ordinary page exposes one accessible keyboard-operable canonical actions menu
Sub-640 px sidebars are modal overlay drawers with safe dismissal
Transient semantic parent branches dismiss their newest live child before their parent
Bare slash defaults to Page reference and continues into an empty page picker
Page and tag completion use the OG adaptive default while preserving explicit existing and typed policies
Mod-L inserts parser-aware Markdown and Org external links through every editor command surface
Linked and unlinked references render in side panels
Page-property backlinks render their linkified value in References, not raw key:: value
Native form controls retain Tab traversal while outline editors keep indentation ownership
Reference results include alias mentions and explicit links in page properties
Bare tags and alias property values contribute canonical Linked References
Mixed-case page links route to the existing canonical page across navigation surfaces
macOS window decorations do not duplicate native-looking corners

coverage.exemption: macOS-only appearance remains a recorded native-platform gap; shared chrome structure is render-tested.

entries.title: macOS does not repeatedly request graph-folder permission

coverage.exemption: The sandbox permission prompt requires a signed macOS application and cannot be reproduced on Linux CI.

entries.title: Multiline nodes do not inherit first-line formatting
Underscores render without unintended emphasis
Journal references use chronological rather than modified order
Custom page properties render wiki-link values
Calendar Today resolves the current day at click time
Entering a sheet cell does not shift its layout
Typed Table cell values commit before Tab navigation and remain visible during the next edit
Multiline paste inside a code fence stays in the block
A top-level block can be deleted without corrupting siblings
Templates can be found and inserted
Template placeholders resolve Logseq-compatible English natural-language dates into configured journal links
entries.notes: OG calls chrono-node 2.2.4 after its named-variable path. Tine lazily prepares that exact parser's supported English locale entry point in both template workflows (not a phrase whitelist), retains an unparseable whole placeholder verbatim, and formats the local-clock result through journalTitle. The narrow import deliberately does not promise non-English Chrono locales.

entries.title: Deleting a page-link opener does not leave unmatched brackets
Windows New page button creates and opens a page
Org block IDs remain hidden property drawers
macOS Command-key wheel tails do not change text size
Closing the Linux app does not crash WebKit subprocesses
Opening All pages avoids unsafe emoji renderer crashes
Clicking right of a trailing page link places the caret after it
The Linux welcome window exposes a working close control
Org hidden properties stay hidden while editing
Journal feed scrolls without requiring a window resize
Shift-clicking a linked page opens the sidebar without selecting text
Windows clipboard screenshots paste as image bytes
Enter on a zoomed leaf creates a child
Android does not offer the unsupported desktop updater
About-page links open through the platform link handler
External web links do not open in a broken in-app browser
Deleting a journal day leaves one editable block
Inline iframe dimensions honor sanitized style attributes
Calculator blocks and results survive blur
The Calculator slash command activates the live calculator immediately
Linked references preserve task markers
Logseq PDF highlight links open without crashing or runaway viewer resource use
PDF area highlights use the OG platform modifier and post-drag color choice
entries.notes: All-platform component coverage plus direct Linux WebKit native proof of Shift and toolbar entry, chooser cancellation/no-write, mode reset, compatible persistence, and reopen. Direct Windows/macOS native gestures remain unverified in this packet.

entries.title: PDF readers expose persistent light warm and dark themes plus nested outline destination navigation
entries.notes: All-platform component coverage plus direct Linux WebKit native proof of accessible light, warm, and dark selection, explicit and named destinations, and a same-XDG process relaunch proving theme persistence. Direct Windows/macOS native presentation remains unverified in this packet.

entries.title: PDF uploads highlights annotation pages and restored view state match Logseq OG while retaining Tine filename templates
entries.notes: Direct literal upload, selection, clipboard, sidecar/HLS and reopen proof is Linux WebKit/GTK; component and Rust contracts cover shared cross-platform behavior. Windows/macOS native upload and chooser surfaces remain tentative.

Mobile pages reserve most of the narrow viewport for outline text
The outline guide toggles all collapsible descendants in one accessible action
Search results separate context from bounded excerpts and highlight all actual positive matches
Graph search can open in a session-backed virtual query tab and materialize safely when named

Friendly and advanced query frontends preserve predicates and explain diagnostics and match evidence
Scrollable panes share quiet theme-aware scrollbar styling with accessible platform fallbacks

Persistent-workspace and inline Filters or Advanced Search result excerpts stay within their pane for large page and block result sets

Large reference results use bounded exact-hit excerpts and accessible page-group disclosure
Ctrl K uses favorites and device-local frecency only to break ties within objective relevance classes
Tine exposes one consistent visible search trigger plus Ctrl K across layouts
Ctrl Enter cycles every selected block independently in one atomic undoable update
Opening fenced code blocks offer bounded language autocomplete without changing bare-fence Enter
Linux Developer Tools detach after attachment on X11/XWayland while native Wayland stays usable docked
Page property settings preserve literal header structure from UI action through guarded save and reload
Canonical Markdown page-header properties use the ordinary editor and persist without an outline bullet
Re-saving a page whose header is the flagless properties-only first bullet never jams the save queue with a page-preamble refusal

Plasma Wayland task switchers resolve Tine's application icon instead of a generic placeholder
Reference badges and panels refresh for already-identified targets and fresh Copy block ref identities remain live and durable

A Linux secondary launch can hand off Quick Capture without an Xlib/XCB thread abort

Voice recording is bounded and Android preserves its native cache token through Kotlin, Rust, and the frontend import

entries.notes: Blocking graph-ownership repair. Proof is semantic filesystem state, not debounce timing or viewer pixels.

entries.title: QueryBuilder popovers dismiss before their Advanced modal parent without changing query drafts
Formula value pickers dismiss one active child before their editor without changing the formula draft
Selection formatting keeps browser-selected outer whitespace outside Markdown and Org delimiters
Inner pane overscroll never rubber-bands fixed application chrome
A line-leading inline-code property lookalike remains visible code rather than hidden metadata
Graph-global Recent follows foreground page activation without promoting background or restored tabs
Children-backed Sheet field rename migrates schema rows and dependent expressions atomically
Linked References combine bounded content search with canonical include and exclude facets
Linked-reference and query hits show one bounded ordered ancestor breadcrumb
Reference and query trees initialize bounded view-local disclosure without mutating source collapse
Search treats canonically equivalent Unicode page names aliases and block text as identical without accent folding
entries.notes: Catalog-derived OG parity finding. Scope is lowercase plus NFC only: regex source bytes remain unchanged and ASCII cafe must not match cafe with an acute accent. Linux native WebDriver proves page/alias/block equivalence, full-grapheme evidence, exact Create suppression, and the accent-folding negative.

entries.title: Shift-only Enter opens selected Ctrl K page and block results in the right sidebar
entries.notes: Catalog-derived OG parity finding; Linux native WebDriver proves literal Shift-only Enter for page and block results while the main route remains unchanged. Mod-Enter transfer and path-addressed duplicate-page sidebar identity are explicitly excluded.

entries.title: An explicitly selected physical page remains the edit owner through follow-on navigation, parked surfaces, file actions, and persisted reopen
entries.notes: BC-H1 high repair on pinned base d4b9a282d6ec95310d51bd061412ba805ef50009. Fail-before command: ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/router.test.ts src/router.recent.test.ts src/session.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts --reporter=verbose (3 causal failures, 37 passing). Known-owner transitions retain one graph-relative PageTarget/BlockTarget; stale or ambiguous mutation targets fail closed. The name-keyed working set may expose one same-name physical owner. Legacy DSL RefGroups, typed links, Favorites, calendar, Guide, and other intentionally logical routes remain pathless.

entries.title: Mod Shift K searches only blocks owned by the focused routed page
entries.notes: Linux native WebDriver proves a collapsed current-page descendant, exclusion of the same query on another page and all global providers, plus no-single-page fallback to global search. Path-aware page identity remains covered below the native layer.

entries.title: An installed plugin with an incompatible manifest remains removable by its real package identity
A cached signed revocation prevents persisted plugin and theme activation before a bounded live refresh
entries.notes: Causal JS/native tests cover one signed envelope, guarded split-key migration, absent versus unsafe startup, activation hold/release, durable cached/live disable with retry, failed-publication last-good preservation, and generation/settings races. The production-key-signed native journey proves atomic migration and restart non-execution and passed three consecutive unchanged-binary release-mode runs on f688b746 (13.1s, 13.2s, 13.1s); it is stable and included in linux-release.

entries.title: Asynchronous plugin results stay owned by the graph and editor generation that invoked them
entries.notes: Exact graph/effect ownership prevents a delayed graph-A command, slash completion, or decoration response from mutating or focusing an equal UUID/raw entity in graph B. On frozen d4b9a282, the causal deferred manager test failed with graph B raw 'A result' instead of 'same raw'. Deterministic unit/render proof is green; the native journey passed three consecutive unchanged-binary release-mode runs on d75f747e (4.9s, 4.8s, 4.8s) and is stable in linux-release.

entries.title: A signed theme installs when its validated mode set matches regardless of canonical key order
Mouse side buttons X1 and X2 navigate page history back and forward
entries.notes: Native mouse side-button navigation (GH #156). WebKitGTK/WebView2 do not deliver X1/X2 to the web layer as auxclick (physical test on fcc8079 confirmed the DOM-only listener was dead in Tine though fine in Firefox), so native_mouse_history.rs intercepts them: Linux GDK button-press 8/9 via with_webview connect_event (stop propagation), Windows WM_APPCOMMAND via window subclassing; both emit a history-navigate Tauri event driving the existing goBack/goForward. The DOM auxclick listener remains a fallback with a 100ms native+DOM de-dup, filtered by window label. Linux PHYSICALLY CONFIRMED by the maintainer on deployed 688a654 (2026-07-18); the Windows path is implemented but unverified on Windows hardware. Unit test covers the DOM/bridge dispatch; the native handler's real proof is the physical test.

entries.title: Pasted block text keeps literal square brackets instead of backslash-escaping them
entries.notes: Martin internal report. Turndown (HTML->Markdown structured paste) escaped Markdown punctuation in text nodes, so copying '[ref]' pasted as backslash-bracket. Fixed by overriding the Turndown instance text escaper to identity in src/editor/htmlPaste.ts; structure (headings/lists/links/code) still emitted by dedicated rules. Fail-before proven in htmlPaste.test.tsx (multi-block outline raw was backslash-escaped before). Accepted side effect: external HTML text literally containing Markdown like double-asterisk bold now round-trips as Markdown rather than escaped literal, which is correct for Tine whose block raw IS Markdown.

entries.title: Deleting a block selection or returning to a pane keeps a block selected and keyboard-navigable
entries.notes: Martin internal report. deleteSelection() and the pane-select return paths (activate/dismiss) unconditionally cleared the block selection, and Arrow block-nav is gated on hasSelection(), so from empty there was no keyboard way back in. Now deleteSelection re-selects a survivor (next visible, else previous, else parent, else clear) and pane-return restores the remembered block (else the focused pane's first visible block). Fail-before proven by manager: 2 store + 2 keybindings tests fail on pre-fix code, pass after; sibling pane-select suite unaffected. Real-app keyboard feel confirmed separately on the deployed binary.

entries.title: Linked/Unlinked References match Logseq: NFC page/alias identity, plain property text as unlinked, same-name merge, real title beats alias, and visible truncation/error
entries.notes: GH #137 matched to LEGACY OG (Logseq) source behavior (not the DB-rewrite master), per Martin's directive to match OG not preference. Page/alias identity = lowercase+NFC+boundary-slash (refs::page_key), applied to aliases and to scoped cache invalidation via a shared real-page-first resolver; plain unbracketed property text (including page-level front matter) counts as Unlinked; same-basename/different-path pages merge into one group keyed by normalized name; a real title is not shadowed by another page's alias and duplicate alias owners resolve by deterministic path sort; panels surface truncation (showing N of M) and a bounded error instead of an empty panel. Adversarially verified (Sol/xhigh, 2 rounds: 3 blockers found+fixed, re-verified CLEAR). Follow-up (non-blocking): an older rejected reference request can set a stale loadError label after a newer success (request-sequencing race).

entries.title: Autocomplete and Ctrl+K rank all matching blocks globally before capping, so a strong match is never omitted by graph traversal order
entries.notes: GH #186. Page/tag candidates were already a global BinaryHeap top-k, but block-ref production walked pages in traversal order and broke at the cap with a flat score, so a strong block match on a late page was silently omitted. Fixed: query_plan::execute_blocks builds a global bounded-heap top-k with real per-block relevance (match-class > word-boundary > earlier-offset > shorter-text > fewer-occurrences, deterministic tie-break by (page rel_path, in-page index) — bare traversal index was nondeterministic because walk_page_files reads fs::read_dir unsorted; regression block_search_topk_ties_sort_rel_path_with_reversed_page_snapshot forces a reversed page snapshot); OG caps adopted (inline pages 100 / block-refs 20; Ctrl+K 100/100 ranked pool, existing chunked presentation); autocomplete fold NFKC->NFC to match #137/backend. Fail-before proven (best block on last page survives + ranks ahead of weaker earlier matches; NFC-vs-NFKC ordering). Manager-reviewed block top-k + green (tine-core 455, autocomplete 50, component 20); not separately adversarially verified (search-ordering, lower risk than #137).

entries.title: Settings panel scrolls on narrow/mobile viewports so every setting is reachable

coverage.exemption: Martin internal report (Android Settings not scrollable). CSS-layout fix: in the mobile column layout (.settings-modal flex-direction:column at max-width:480px) .settings-pane lacked min-height:0, so it grew to its content height and .settings-pane-body's overflow-y:auto scroll never engaged, leaving lower settings clipped by the modal's overflow:hidden — the textbook flexbox min-height:auto scroll bug. Fixed by adding min-height:0 to .settings-pane and .settings-pane-body. jsdom cannot exercise CSS layout, and headless-Chromium navigation to Settings in the mobile drawer layout could not be automated here (confirmed the app DOES enter the mobile column layout at narrow viewports). Needs mobile-viewport/device confirmation, pending with the other Android items (#192, M-B).

entries.title: The mobile sidebar drawer closes on a horizontal swipe toward its edge, distinct from a vertical scroll
entries.notes: Martin internal report (M-B). MobileDrawerPanel now has touchstart/move/end handlers that, on the first ~10px of movement, distinguish a horizontal drag toward the drawer's closed edge (left drawer -> drag left; right -> drag right) from a vertical scroll or an opening-ward drag; a close-ward drag past max(56px, 30% of panel width) calls dismiss('back'). Synthetic-touch test proves a close-ward swipe dismisses while vertical, short, and opening-ward drags do not (red pre-fix: no touch handlers existed). Live finger-following animation deferred as polish. The touch FEEL still needs mobile-device confirmation.

entries.title: Accepting a page/block-ref completion while editing inside an existing ref rewrites the whole ref, leaving no stray closing bracket (Logseq parity)
entries.notes: GH #199. Editing inside an already-closed [[word]] (e.g. autopaired [[second]], arrow-left, type 'first ' -> [[first |second]]) and accepting a completion left a stray ]] ([[first]]second]]) because Block.tsx replaceTrigger only swallowed the closing pair when it sat IMMEDIATELY at the caret (ref.value.slice(t.end,t.end+2)===pair). OG-verified (og@6e7afa8, findings in tine-agents specs notes): OG's query range is [[->caret (matches Tine's detectTrigger, unchanged), but on accept OG scans forward from the caret to the ref's closer (:end-pattern ]]), strips it (postfix-fn) and rewrites the whole region. Fix: new pure refCompletionEnd(value,end,insertedText) in autocomplete.ts scans forward on the CURRENT LINE for the closer matching the completion's suffix (]]/))/}}), returning just past it or the caret when none ahead; replaceTrigger now uses it. Covers page AND block refs (same latent defect) and the latent end-caret ]]]] doubling. Not a regression: latent since the autocomplete feature shipped (e54ad36e, 2026-06-14). Fail-before proven (pre-fix immediate-only range yields [[first]]second]]); 7 unit cases (mid/end/unclosed/block/line-bounded/non-ref/reporter-integration); Block.referenceAuthoring + tagAutocomplete + selectionWrap render tests still green (wiring regression guard). Delegated to gpt-5.6-terra (xhigh), manager-reviewed + gates run. Real-app editor confirmation pending on next deploy (pure-logic fix, proportionate).

entries.title: Linked References hide the per-block mention count and occurrence-jump buttons unless a block mentions the page more than once (Logseq parity)
entries.notes: GH #200. Each backlink block rendered Tine's OccurrenceControls (ReferenceEvidence.tsx) always-on (LiveRefGroup.tsx + reference excerpt rows): a '{n} mention(s)' label plus one numbered occurrence-jump button per mention. For a single-mention block (the common case) this is a redundant '1 mention' + lone '1' button, which the reporter found confusing. OG (logseq@6e7afa8) shows NEITHER a per-block mention count nor occurrence-jump buttons in Linked References (only the separate ((block))-ref-count badge, which Tine also has and which correctly shows nothing for a non-block-referenced block). Per Martin's decision (option B), OccurrenceControls now renders only when occurrences.length>1 — removing the redundancy for single mentions while keeping jump-to-occurrence where multiple mentions make it useful. Render test: 1 mention -> no .reference-occurrence-controls / no 'mention' text; 3 mentions -> '3 mentions' + 3 jump buttons. Fail-before proven (pre-fix rendered controls unconditionally). LinkedReferences/UnlinkedReferences/LiveRefGroup render tests green.

entries.title: A Show brackets setting and mod-c mod-b shortcut toggle whether page-reference [[ ]] brackets render, persisted in config.edn (Logseq parity)
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (settings.show-brackets, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). OG (logseq@6e7afa8) has :ui/show-brackets? in logseq/config.edn defaulting true (state.cljs:652); shortcut :ui/toggle-brackets = mod+c mod+b (shortcut/config.cljs:362) flips it; a Show brackets appearance toggle (settings.cljs:209); page-reference render gates the .bracket spans on state/show-brackets? (block.cljs:759/973/1193), with nested-link outer and the contents page and html-export as exceptions. Tine had no such setting/shortcut and always rendered brackets (inline.tsx:310). Implemented full parity mirroring :feature/enable-timetracking? end-to-end (config.rs field+writer, model.rs GraphMeta, commands.rs/lib.rs command, backend.ts/mock.ts, ui.ts showBrackets/changeShowBrackets, inline.tsx render gate, keybindings.ts, Settings.tsx toggle). Fail-before: showBrackets.test.tsx asserts brackets vanish when show_brackets:false (red before the render gate existed).

entries.title: Property names are folded (lowercase + space/underscore to hyphen) for display and matching so done_at and done-at are the same property, without rewriting the file (Logseq parity)
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (property.name-normalization, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). OG (docs Properties.md:5-13 @08f855f; graph_parser/block.cljs:212-216 @680b371) folds a property key: lower-case then replace ' ' and '_' with '-', so done_at is the same property as done-at, and the rendered/effective name is the folded one. Tine (pre-fix on 197b226) compares keys with eq_ignore_ascii_case only (case fold, NO underscore fold) and renders the key verbatim (body.tsx renderProps), so done_at stays a distinct key from done-at and a done-at query/hidden-prop/alias match misses done_at::. Martin chose the DATA-SAFE fold: normalize the effective key for display + all matching, but never rewrite the file. Fail-before proven (manager-run): DocBlock::property("done-at") on `Done_At:: 1` returned None pre-fix; the round-trip of `Done_At:: 1` was already byte-exact and must stay so. Non-goals (follow-up): custom_id/custom-id->id and last-modified-at->updated-at alias remaps.

entries.title: Ctrl K shows a truncated-results affordance instead of implying a bounded result window is complete
entries.notes: Blocking stateful UX: each truncated Ctrl+K page or block category tells the user to narrow the query instead of implying the bounded window is complete. Existing result caps remain unchanged. (Autocomplete keep-typing hint deferred: it would require rerouting the [[/#/(( picker from QueryPlan::legacy_page_search to friendly, an unverified ranking change out of scope for GH #209.)

entries.title: A bare http(s) URL ending in a media extension auto-renders inline (image as , video/audio as player) like Logseq, while labeled links stay links
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (og-parity.assets-pdf-media.raw-remote-image-url, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). OG (og-1.0.0 components/block.cljs:989-1011 show-link? -> :1213 media-link; util/text.cljs:31 media-link?; formats config.cljs:118-127 + graph_parser/config.cljs:66) auto-renders a link as media when full_text starts with http(s):// AND the URL ends in a media extension (?query/#frag allowed): images gif/svg/jpeg/ico/png/jpg/bmp/webp, audio mp3/ogg/mpeg/wav/m4a/flac/wma/aac, video mp4/webm/mov/flv/avi/mkv. Tine renderLink (src/render/inline.tsx) rendered media only when the lsdoc node had image:true (![](url) syntax); a BARE https://host/pic.jpg link fell through to a plain <a class=external-link> (pre-fix). Fix: when !s.image AND s.full (raw source = OG full_text) starts with http(s):// AND mediaKind(dest)!=null, route to AssetImage (image) / MediaEmbed (video/audio) -- the components the s.image branch already uses. Discriminator on s.full (not label emptiness) is byte-faithful to OG: a labeled [text](x.png) has full '[text](x.png)' (not http-prefixed) and STAYS a link. Pure render change, no write path. Extension set = Tine's canonical mediaKind (media.ts) rather than OG's literal list (Tine adds avif/m4v/oga/opus; lacks ico) -- an intentional harmless presentation-heuristic difference, noted per AGENTS parity doctrine. Fail-before proven (manager-run).

entries.title: A {{youtube}}/{{video}} embed (and a pasted raw <iframe> to a video host) plays instead of failing with YouTube error 153; arbitrary pasted iframes keep no-referrer
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (og-parity.assets-pdf-media.youtube-embed-playback, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). A YouTube embed iframe that loads with NO referrer (or an opaque one) is rejected by YouTube's player as error 153. OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb extensions/video/youtube.cljs:54-70) builds the youtube iframe as https://www.youtube.com/embed/ID?enablejsapi=1 with allow='accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share' and referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' so the app origin reaches YouTube; vimeo (block.cljs:1290-1305) gets the same allow minus picture-in-picture/web-share and no referrerpolicy. Pre-fix Tine: VideoMacro (src/components/Macro.tsx) emitted a bare <iframe src=.../embed/ID allowfullscreen> with no referrerpolicy/allow/enablejsapi, and renderIframe (src/render/inline.tsx, the raw-HTML <iframe> path) hard-coded referrerpolicy='no-referrer' (sends nothing). Fix: VideoMacro youtube gets ?enablejsapi=1 + the OG allow list + referrerpolicy strict-origin-when-cross-origin; vimeo gets the OG allow list (no referrerpolicy); renderIframe sends strict-origin-when-cross-origin ONLY for known video hosts (youtube/youtube-nocookie/youtu.be/vimeo/loom/bilibili) and keeps no-referrer for every other pasted iframe (privacy preserved). Structural DOM parity proven by render tests (fail-before manager-run); actual WebKitGTK+network playback confirmation is real-app-pending (jsdom cannot load YouTube).

entries.title: A #+BEGIN_QUERY advanced query block renders its authored title and bounded result table (never raw delimiters/payload) on the main page, hydrated linked references, and the RefBlocks fallback; malformed or unsupported payloads fail visibly and safely
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (og-parity.queries.begin-query-linked-references, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb) parses #+BEGIN_QUERY as Custom/query and dispatches it to the custom-query component (components/block.cljs:3278-3284) which evaluates and renders title + result table (components/query.cljs:171-247), including inside Linked References subtrees (block.cljs:2811-2815,2985-2992). Pre-fix Tine rendered the block as raw source on all three surfaces (generic custom renderer in body.tsx; InlineText lines in RefBlocks). Fix: lsdoc-confirmed whole-block custom/query dispatches to BeginQuery.tsx which slices the authored EDN from raw, extracts :title/:query, and reuses the bounded QueryMacro path read-only with strictAdvanced (ignored clauses suppress partial rows instead of guessing); malformed payloads render a visible Unsupported BEGIN_QUERY alert. Rust lowers ONLY the exact paired [?p :block/properties ?props] + [(get ?props :key)] DataScript form to the existing bounded PageProperty predicate (positive+negative tests). Static publish deliberately unchanged (open product decision, sweep questions Q2). Necessity gate manager-run: 5/6 BeginQuery tests fail pre-fix (non-query custom guard passes); Rust pair-lowering test fails pre-fix (worker-run). jsdom-layer proof; real-app confirmation pending.

entries.title: The Copy/export modal offers OG's maximum-depth control (Level <= all/1..9) and the text serializer omits descendants deeper than the selected level
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (export.options, LIVE-BACKLOG s9, slice 1 of the export parity work). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb) defaults :keep-only-level<=N to :all (handler/export/common.cljs:45-54) and prunes deeper headings before serialization (common.cljs:547-565); the modal exposes all/1..9 (components/export.cljs:262-275). Pre-fix Tine ExportOptions had no depth field and exportOutline visited every child unconditionally. Fix: maxDepth option (default all), guarded traversal (roots = depth 1), Level <= selector in ExportModal for Rendered+Source. Necessity gate manager-run: 3 new depth tests fail pre-fix. Remaining export parity residuals (OPML, fragment HTML, page route, page PNG) tracked in sweep questions Q3/Q4.

entries.title: Typing :: at a fresh line (or a typed key prefix ending ::) opens a property-name picker of graph-known OG-visible keys; choosing a key inserts canonical `key:: ` and immediately offers that key's known values; :: in prose/references/code fences never triggers
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (property.autocomplete, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): name trigger handler/editor.cljs:1907-1924; key->value transition editor.cljs:2211-2226; candidates db/model.cljs:1394-1405,1422-1443 + search.cljs:184-215; hidden-key policy util/property.cljs:18-24. Pre-fix Tine detectTrigger had no property branch at all. Fix: property-name/property-value trigger kinds in src/editor/autocomplete.ts (line-anchored key alphabet, fence-guarded, value stage only after this editor selected its key); two-stage picker in Block.tsx (fuzzy rank, Create entries, canonical fold via propertyKeyNorm — single spelling policy); dedicated bounded backend boundary query_facets{autocomplete:true} -> autocomplete_property_facets_bounded (includes page preambles + template/title, excludes OG hidden built-ins + graph :block-hidden-properties, item/byte budget) leaving query-builder facets untouched. Necessity gate manager-run: 3 trigger tests + the replace-span/value-flow component test fail pre-fix; the prose/fence no-trigger guard passes pre-fix. jsdom-layer proof; real-app confirm pending.

entries.title: A configured :macros macro whose body is (or contains) a Hiccup form renders rich sanitized output (e.g. a styled span), not the literal [:tag ...] source, on the main page, hydrated linked refs/query/embed, and the RefBlocks fallback; direct non-macro Hiccup stays literal
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (macro.rich-output, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). High tier (graph-authored EDN -> live DOM). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): macro bodies re-parsed (block.cljs:955-967,1379-1441); Hiccup safe-read -> hiccups.core/html -> SANITIZED (block.cljs:1554-1562,1617-1621,3266-3271). Contract deliberately NARROWED to Tine's ADR-0019 sanitizer policy (no promise of iframe/id/style/unknown-protocol survival; OG sanitizes with a looser DOMPurify config, security.cljs:5-11). Spec adversarially verified by Sol/xhigh (v1 REVISE: iframe fast-path bypass blocker; v2 APPROVE). Design: data-only bounded transcriber src/render/hiccup.ts (validated tag/attr tokens re-emitted, all text/attr values escaped, whole-conversion literal fallback on any unsupported/malformed form, parse-time bounds depth 64 / 2048 nodes / 64KB) -> HTML string -> the EXTRACTED sanitizer-only renderSanitizedHtml path (raw-HTML iframe fast-path unreachable from Hiccup; zero new innerHTML sites); immutable macroExpansion flag threaded through all recursive inline/block rendering, set ONLY by UserMacroView. Direct Hiccup literal behavior guarded by existing + mixed-render tests. Hostile cases (script/onerror/javascript:/iframe nested+top/markup-in-text/attr breakout) tested. Necessity gate manager-run: 2 surface + 5 macro-helper tests fail pre-fix. jsdom-layer proof; real-app confirm pending. Static export unchanged (needs its own frozen contract).

entries.title: The Copy/export modal offers Text/OPML/HTML (no PNG) for both a block selection and a page (via the page menu), with OG's per-format option scoping; OPML/HTML omit properties unconditionally and escape all graph text
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (export.content-formats + export.options residual slices, LIVE-BACKLOG s9, Martin-approved Q4). OG (og-1.0.0): page menu routes into the same export modal (page_menu.cljs:139-143); Text/OPML/HTML for any target, PNG page-only (export.cljs:148-168); per-format options (indentation/property/newline Text-only :190-206,240-260; brackets/emphasis/tags shared :207-238; max depth shared :262-275); OPML/HTML omit properties unconditionally (opml.cljs:404-410; html.cljs:386-392). Pre-fix Tine: modal was Text-only (Rendered/Source) and the page menu had no export route. Fix: pure ExportNode->OPML-2.0 and ->nested-HTML-fragment serializers (exportOpml.ts/exportHtml.ts over shared exportMarkup.ts; XML text+attribute escaping, serializer-owned emphasis tags only, publish_html NOT involved); format switcher + per-format option scoping in ExportModal; page context menu opens the modal with the page root forest (Copy-page-as-Markdown + PDF actions unchanged). Necessity gate manager-run: 5 component tests fail pre-fix (page route + format controls); serializer suites are new-module coverage. Residuals: Copy-only (no save-to-file boundary yet); PNG absent everywhere (page PNG = WONTFIX Jul 21). jsdom-layer proof; real-app confirm pending.

entries.title: A page or block bearing `url:: value` appears in the `url` page's Linked References by virtue of the property KEY alone (value refs are independent), with OG's eligibility rules (built-in/hidden keys and :property-pages/excludelist create no membership; :property-pages/enabled? false disables it; self page excluded)
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (og-parity.properties-config.property-key-page-backlinks.url, frozen class A). NOT co-fixed by property-value-backlinks (that covers [[ref]] IN values). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb graph_parser/block.cljs:134-188): eligible property NAMES create page refs independently of value refs, enabled when :property-pages/enabled? is true or absent, minus excludelist and the exact editable+hidden built-in sets (property.cljs:46-108 + srs.cljs:763-769 card-* extension keys) — all transcribed verbatim into reference_evidence.rs and compared via property_key_norm. Pre-fix Tine's ReferenceSourceProjection kept only property VALUE offsets, so `url:: https://x` created no membership (replay: backlinks_bounded returned only the value-ref control). Fix: explicit_property_key occurrence at the KEY's source span for both page preambles and block properties; config.rs parses :property-pages/enabled?+excludelist. Fail-before (worker-run, Sol/high): key-membership test fails pre-fix with only the value-ref witness present. Full tine-core 514 pass (manager-run). Known bounded limitation: scoped cache invalidation uses default-enabled policy (can only over-invalidate, never stale) — config-exact invalidation is a follow-up. Rust-layer proof; live LinkedReferences surface confirm pending.

entries.title: 'Toggle undo/redo mode' palette command switches history between Global (default) and Page only; page-only undo/redo removes only the newest entry touching the active page, leaving other pages' history and content untouched
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 14 (frozen receipt 014--history-global-page-modes). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): :history/page-only-mode? defaults false (state.cljs:304-306); filtered newest-entry pop (undo_redo.cljs:81-106,132-156); editing-page-over-route-page precedence (util/page.cljs:14-29); palette toggle w/ empty binding + mode report (undo_redo.cljs:232-237, shortcut/config.cljs:355-356). Pre-fix Tine had a single global stack, no mode. Fail-before (manager-run park/restore): 5/5 historyParity tests fail on pre-fix tree. Data-safety: interleaved A/B edits + repeated undo/redo leave untouched page's pageToDto byte-identical. jsdom proof; real-app E2E for palette toast + cross-pane behavior pending.

entries.title: Undo/redo restores the pre-edit UI context (route/pane, right-sidebar open state and items, editing block focus with clamped selection) in Global mode; Page-only mode restores editor context without route changes; a missing route/block degrades safely without losing the applied data inverse
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 15 (frozen receipt 015--history-restore-context). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): entries capture route/sidebar app state (undo_redo.cljs:261-272) + pre-tx editor cursor (outliner/datascript.cljc:152-162); restore order and global-mode route gating (handler/history.cljs:10-60). Pre-fix Tine entries carried page/node/text only and replay unconditionally ended editing. Fix: HistoryContext payload on every raw/snapshot entry; guarded route/pane/sidebar adapters (router.ts/panes.ts/ui.ts); controller-owned textarea registration + clamped selection restore (editorController.ts/Block.tsx). INVARIANT: data replay + opposite-stack push complete before any best-effort UI restoration; UI failure cannot discard/reorder the inverse (degraded-path test proves exact DTO restore with deleted block/route). jsdom proof; native focus/selection after remounts needs real-app E2E.

entries.title: In Document mode, plain Enter inserts an internal newline and Shift+Enter creates a new block (inverted from outline mode), with a config escape hatch (:shortcut/doc-mode-enter-for-new-block?) restoring Enter-as-new-block; outline-mode behavior unchanged
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 2 (frozen receipt 002--editor-internal-line-break). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): doc-mode handler inversion handler/editor.cljs:2521-2533 + state.cljs:714-717; escape-hatch key common_config.cljc:41. Pre-fix Tine's Document mode was appearance-only. Setting persisted as durable graph config.edn boolean (not WebKitGTK localStorage). Fail-before (manager park/restore): inversion test fails pre-fix (expected alpha\n got alpha); default-mapping + escape-hatch guard tests pass pre-fix by design. jsdom proof; native key dispatch/caret needs real-app E2E.

entries.title: Editor setting 'Logical outdenting' (default off = traditional): when on, Shift+Tab moves the block after its parent leaving following siblings in place; when off, following siblings are reparented under the outdented block (current behavior preserved)
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 5 (frozen receipt 005--editor-logical-outdenting). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): :editor/logical-outdenting? common_config.cljc:83, settings toggle components/settings.cljs:444-453,731-748, tree mutation modules/outliner/core.cljs:835-852. Branch lives inside shared store outdentBlock so all callers honor it. Durable graph config.edn boolean; round-trip proven via fresh Graph reopen (tine-core graph.rs). Fail-before (manager park/restore): logical-mode tree test fails pre-fix (siblings reparented). Byte-exact JSON serialization of untouched siblings asserted. jsdom proof; real-app Shift+Tab caret needs E2E.

entries.title: Typing exactly '1. ' in an empty block turns it into a numbered-list block (logseq.order-list-type:: number) and clears the trigger text; 'x1. ' or '1. x' stay literal; the context-menu toggle uses the same operation
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 8 (frozen receipt 008--editor-numbered-list-toggle). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): exact-value trigger + editor clear handler/editor.cljs:1888-1892; property toggle handler/editor.cljs:74-104. Own-numbered state IS the property, never inferred from visible '1.' text. Property writes go through audited splitProps/joinProps (org gets :PROPERTIES: drawer); hidden props (id::) preserved when trigger text is cleared. Fail-before (manager park/restore): exact-trigger + org-trigger tests fail pre-fix; non-exact literal controls pass by design. jsdom proof; native InputEvent/caret needs real-app E2E.

entries.title: Enter on a numbered-list block makes the new sibling numbered; Enter on an EMPTY non-nested numbered block stops the list (removes the property, no new block); Backspace at offset 0 removes only the property keeping the text — in-block markdown lists keep their separate behavior
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 9 (frozen receipt 009--editor-numbered-list-continuation). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): empty non-nested guard handler/editor.cljs:2498-2502; offset-zero Backspace property removal handler/editor.cljs:2752-2764; split inheritance outliner/core.cljs:420-433. Split-inheritance already existed (kept as existing coverage, not claimed as necessity). Fail-before (manager park/restore): empty-Enter stop + Backspace property-removal tests fail pre-fix (unwanted sibling created / block merged away); plain-block Backspace merge + in-block markdown list controls pass by design. jsdom proof; caret journey needs real-app E2E.

entries.title: Dragging or structurally pasting an untyped block next to/under a numbered-list target gives it the numbered property (in memory and serialized, org drawer included); an already-numbered source keeps its own property when moved to a plain target
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 10 (frozen receipt 010--editor-numbered-list-drag-paste). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): preserve-or-inherit rule outliner/core.cljs:420-433 applied in the common insert path :536-555. One shared rawWithInheritedOrderListType helper used by moveBlock (drop-target-aware via new dropTargetId param) and all three structural outline insertion paths. Fail-before (manager park/restore): 4 store tests fail pre-fix (drag, sibling paste, multi-root paste, org drawer). Untouched siblings byte-identical in memory + pageToDto. jsdom/unit proof; native pointer drag + rendered ordinal need real-app E2E.

entries.title: Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+V pastes the clipboard's literal plain text for EVERY payload kind — single-line rich HTML, selected bare URL, multiline — replacing the selection with no outline/link/image/file interpretation; empty plain flavor claims the gesture and changes nothing
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 12 (frozen receipt 012--clipboard-raw-paste). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): editor-on-paste-raw! handler/paste.cljs:262-271 — delete selection, insert clipboard text, no branches. Pre-fix Tine honored the latch only for multiline text; single-line rich HTML became an outline and a selected bare URL became a link. Raw branch now runs first in onPaste, before file/sheet/HTML/link/video/image decisions; splice is byte-exact prefix+text+suffix. Fail-before (manager park/restore): rich-HTML, selected-URL, empty-flavor tests fail pre-fix; multiline raw controls pass by design. jsdom synthetic ClipboardEvent; WebKitGTK flavor ordering/permissions need real-app smoke.

entries.title: Formatted paste keeps HTML images (as ![alt](src) / org [[src][alt]], declining non-base64 data: URLs without dropping surrounding text), and pasting a bare YouTube/Loom/Vimeo/Bilibili URL with no selection inserts {{video URL}}; selected-URL link-wrap keeps precedence
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 11 remainder (frozen receipt 011--clipboard-formatted-paste; lists/tables/links already co-fixed by GH #58). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): image emission + non-base64 data:-URL decline html_parser.cljs:149-158; video providers util/text.cljs:12-23 (regexes transcribed verbatim); wrap + precedence handler/paste.cljs:49-57,137-146,164-166. img/src/alt added to the bounded DOMPurify allow-list; Turndown safeImage rule format-aware; declined image contributes no syntax but never removes adjacent text. {{video}} render compatibility verified vs Macro.tsx video sniffing. Fail-before (manager park/restore): 6 provider URLs + mixed/img-only/org-image tests fail pre-fix; selected-URL precedence control passes by design. Remaining row-11 gaps tracked separately: multiline plain-text policy (ledger Q5), private web application/logseq flavor (ledger Q6).

entries.title: 'Auto' heading (heading:: true) renders at min(nesting depth + 1, 6) and follows indent/outdent reactively; an explicit ATX/numeric heading always wins; Auto is offered in the context menu and slash menu, and transitions clear the incompatible representation in Markdown and Org
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue lane outliner-editor row 7 (frozen receipt 007--editor-heading-auto). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): render precedence + depth rule components/block.cljs:1948-1959; transition clearing handler/editor.cljs:3822-3862; markdown prefix set/clear commands.cljs:623-638; org property path handler/editor.cljs:888-904. Pre-fix Tine mapped heading:: true to hard-coded H1 and the DTO path discarded the boolean (facetsFromDto dropped it). One shared effectiveHeadingLevel resolver serves Block, RefBlocks (query/ref/embed fallbacks), and SheetGrid — closes the known two-facet-renderer divergence risk for headings. No Rust change: BlockDto.properties already carried the parsed state. Fail-before (manager park/restore): 9 render + 3 slash tests fail pre-fix; explicit-precedence control passed pre-fix by design (existing behavior). Serialization byte-exact for both formats; untouched siblings byte-identical. jsdom proof; native menu/caret polish needs real-app E2E.

entries.title: Default paste of multiline plain text follows Logseq's decision tree: markdown/org block-looking text (-/+/*/# lines, org stars) parses into blocks; blank-line-separated paragraphs become trimmed blocks; ordinary prose with single newlines or indentation stays literally inside the current block replacing the selection
entries.notes: Martin decision 2026-07-21 (ledger Q5): adopt OG behavior, replacing GH #58's parse-everything multiline policy. OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): block-looking regexes + fallback order handler/paste.cljs:101-107,168-177; (?:\r?\n){2,} paragraph split + trim :34-47,173-174; literal selection replacement :120-123,176-177. OG quirk transcribed: numbered '1.' lines are NOT block-looking (stay literal). Raw latch, structured-HTML, file/sheet/video/link precedence and fence/calc surfaces unchanged. One old-policy assertion relaxed (latch-abort test: two roots -> one literal block; audited in .replay-notes/P5-impl-report.md). Fail-before (manager park/restore): 6/23 fail pre-fix. jsdom proof; WebKitGTK flavor ordering/persistence needs real-app smoke.

entries.title: Calc code blocks implement Logseq's full calc language: hex/octal/binary literals and :hex/:oct/:bin/:decimal output bases, scientific and mixed-number literals, inverse trig and factorial semantics, :format directives (fix/sci/normal/fractions/improper), exact decimal precision, and failure-to-last error behavior
entries.notes: OG-parity row render.calculator (receipt 034; frozen witness 1+2=3 already passed - this covers the full language). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): grammar calc.bnf:1-52; BigNumber eval + directives + last calc.cljc:41-215. Dependency-free BigInt decimal model. Tine superset kept deliberately: floor/ceil/round remain supported (OG errors on them) so existing graphs don't break. Fail-before (manager park/restore): 6/20 fail pre-fix; 20/20 after. Uncovered legal forms listed in .replay-notes/CA1-impl-report.md.

entries.title: In Org pages, a page reference targeting a local image asset (e.g. [[../assets/pic.png]]) renders as the image itself, matching Logseq; ordinary page refs, markdown pages, and pdf/mp4/ogg/webm targets keep their existing routing
entries.notes: OG-parity row render.inline-org (receipt 017, class A). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): show-link? block.cljs:989-1011 (^[./]*assets or media suffix, graph_parser/config.cljs:18-21,66-68, frontend/config.cljs:116-127); Page_ref image-link branch block.cljs:1135-1159 excluding exactly pdf/mp4/ogg/webm. Format threaded through AstBody/renderBlocks/renderInlines/renderLink + InlineText (defaults md; markdown unchanged). Fail-before (manager park/restore): org-image test fails pre-fix (69/70), 70/70 after. jsdom proof (AssetImage dispatch + img emission, blob I/O mocked); real-app visual confirm pending. Follow-up: static export publish.rs:909-916,1325-1331 does not inherit this TS fix.

entries.title: PDF annotation sidecars written by Tine match Logseq's field shape (new text highlights omit the nil :image key; new area highlights write the "[:span]" text sentinel), and deleting an area highlight moves its cropped PNG to recoverable trash instead of orphaning it
entries.notes: OG-parity rows media.pdf-text-highlight/media.pdf-area-highlight follow-ups (receipts 031/032; primary journeys already pass - frozen receipts were obsolete, see .replay-notes/R1-pdf.md). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): text map core.cljs:636-653 (no :image key); area sentinel core.cljs:491-506; PNG delete-with-highlight core.cljs:155-159 + assets.cljs:137-147. DATA-SAFETY: cleanup runs only AFTER the paired .edn+hls commit, compare-guarded against external writers, moves to typed asset trash (never hard-deletes), rolls back on race, keeps PNG when another highlight shares the stamp. Legacy shapes (:image nil, :text "") still parse. Fail-before (manager park/restore): new round-trip + lifecycle tests fail pre-fix; cargo 518 green after. Real-app PDF E2E journeys exist separately (e2e-pdf-logseq).

entries.title: Direct inline and block Hiccup (e.g. [:span.cls "text"]) renders as sanitized HTML elements like Logseq instead of literal source, and raw HTML audio/video with controls survives sanitization as native playback elements; invalid Hiccup still falls back to literal source
entries.notes: OG-parity row render.hiccup-html (receipt 025, class A; the 40e2c6b transcriber covered only macro-expanded hiccup). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): safe-read+serialize+DOMPurify block.cljs:1554-1562,1617-1626,3258-3271; sanitizer security.cljs:5-11. SECURITY policy delta (audited): +audio/video/source tags, +controls/loop/muted/preload/poster/type attrs; autoplay/on*/script/iframe/object/embed/javascript: stay dead. data: policy = DOMPurify/OG exactly: allowed in media src (base64 images keep working), stripped from poster and from a[href] (manager delta reversed the worker's blanket data: ban, which would have regressed embedded base64 images vs both OG and prior Tine; Rust attribute_filter now mirrors DOMPurify per-attribute behavior). Fail-before (manager park/restore): 9/99 fail pre-fix (TS); Rust necessity via TS-shared policy (colocated tests can't park separately). jsdom structural proof; real playback not exercised.

entries.title: Typing < at the start of a line opens Logseq's advanced-command menu (Quote, Src, Query, Note, Tip, Important, Caution, Pinned, Warning, Example, Export/Latex/Ascii, Center, Verse, Comment); selecting one inserts the paired #+BEGIN/#+END section with the caret placed to continue typing, and on Markdown pages Src inserts a ``` fence like Logseq
entries.notes: OG-parity row render.advanced-sections (receipt 022; rendering of authored sections already passed - the missing part was the < creation journey). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): angle-bracket commands.cljs:30-33; ->block :155-180 (md Src -> fence, Src caret at end of opening line, others on blank middle line); command set :188-218 (Properties excluded - uses ->properties). Intentional Tine difference (documented): trigger only at logical line start, not after any typed < like OG handler/editor.cljs:1901-1905 - mid-word < stays literal. Fail-before: detectTrigger null + component menu empty pre-fix (worker) + md-Src fence test added by manager delta. jsdom caret proof; real-app caret confirm pending.

entries.title: YouTube timestamps work like Logseq: {{youtube-timestamp N}} renders as a clickable stamp that seeks the matching embedded YouTube video, and the 'Embed Youtube timestamp' slash command inserts the current playback time (floored); with no ready player the command inserts nothing and the stamp stays a plain label
entries.notes: OG-parity row media.youtube-timestamps (receipt 028). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): IFrame API load/register youtube.cljs:20-27,45-70; player selection = last preceding YouTube iframe in DOM order :85-101; click seekTo(seconds,true) :103-111; floored generator :113-115; no-player = insert NOTHING :113-122 (manager delta - worker had a 0-fallback); slash command commands.cljs:294-300,669-680. IFrame API script loads lazily only when a YouTube embed mounts; load failure degrades to the plain label. Fail-before (manager park/restore): 4/6 fail pre-fix. jsdom proof with mocked YT.Player; real playback/seek needs the running app. HTML export path untouched (textual).

entries.title: A table in a block with logseq.table.version:: 2 renders Logseq's v2 grid presentation (compact mode, header transforms) instead of the plain markdown table, and the logseq.table.* configuration properties are hidden from the rendered property chrome; v1/default and query tables unchanged
entries.notes: OG-parity row render.table-v2 (receipt 021). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): version resolution shui.cljs:12-23 (block prop > graph config > v1); dispatch block.cljs:3075-3079; v2 grid + header/compact semantics deps/shui/src/logseq/shui/table/v2.cljs:37-50,166-189,364-372. Presentation-only. Follow-ups recorded: graph-config :logseq.table/version fallback needs GraphMeta plumbing (block-property-only today); column widths/colors/hover out of scope. Fail-before (manager park/restore): 4/6 fail pre-fix. jsdom semantic-DOM proof; grid geometry needs screenshot replay.

entries.title: Admonition blocks (NOTE/TIP/IMPORTANT/CAUTION/WARNING/PINNED) render with a per-type icon like Logseq, and any other #+BEGIN_X custom block keeps a wrapper carrying its name as a CSS class (BEGIN_VERSE gets .verse) instead of losing its styling hook; Comment stays hidden, Example stays a code block
entries.notes: OG-parity row render.admonitions (receipt 024). OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb): six-type dispatch block.cljs:3286-3302 (incl. pinned); icon mapping + .admonition-icon structure ui.cljs:825-840; named custom wrapper block.cljs:3309-3313; Comment empty :3352-3353. Tine icons via Twemoji with aria-labels (per-type distinction preserved; exact SVG art is intentional presentation difference). Example-as-CodeBlock kept (deliberate Tine presentation; OG uses plain pre). Skeleton-drift normalizer: .admonition-icon added to frontend-chrome REMOVE_SELECTORS (manager edit - static Rust render keeps the text title; documented mechanism). Fail-before (manager park/restore): 3/82 fail pre-fix; render suite 892 green after. jsdom hook/aria proof; icon paint needs screenshot.

entries.title: Cutting blocks (Ctrl/Cmd+X or context Cut) and pasting them back preserves their id:: identity so ((uuid)) block refs and embeds keep resolving; copy-paste inside the app is block-exact (hidden properties like collapsed::, exact raw text, full subtree) while stripping id:: so copy never duplicates identity; block identity never leaks to the OS clipboard and no paste can create duplicate ids
entries.notes: CB1 workstream (ledger Q6, Martin 2026-07-21). OG parity: og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb copy writes id-stripped text (common.cljs:12-16) + private flavor (util.cljc:842-851); paste checks payload first (paste.cljs:179-191); keep-uuid? is cut-only from INTERNAL op-type (paste.cljs:184-190), cleared after paste (editor.cljs:2072-2076). Tine transport differs intentionally (recorded): app-internal per-webview slot + normalized text/plain association instead of OG's Chromium-only web custom format — clipboard flavors stay byte-identical, no id/graph-path leak, grant unforgeable. Data-safety: 3-round adversarial spec loop (.replay-notes/CB1-specverify*-report.md); cut-source retirement flush w/ page-instance generations; whole-graph resolveBlocks absence check fail-closed; final no-await check-and-insert; one-shot grant; refuse-redo on id conflict. Native WebKitGTK text round-trip probe green (scripts/e2e-clipboard-roundtrip.mjs). Cross-session/cross-webview identity paste intentionally not provided.

entries.title: The /Today slash command inserts a link in the graph's configured journal date format, so it resolves to the real journal page
entries.notes: Fixed by routing the slash action through journal.journalTitle() (fed from :journal/page-title-format) instead of a hardcoded "MMM do, yyyy" formatter.

entries.title: On Windows, clicking an external http(s)/mailto link opens the default browser or mail client, not a File Explorer window
entries.notes: The Windows arm of open_external spawned `explorer <url>`, which treats its argument as a shell namespace item; it now routes through tauri-plugin-opener (open::that_detached -> ShellExecuteExW, feature shellexecute-on-windows confirmed on in the vendored crate). Evidence is structural plus vendored-source reading plus a hosted windows-scope CI compile/core-test run on the batch branch; NOT confirmed on a real Windows desktop, so the claim stays tentative until the reporter or a Windows run verifies it. Unrelated to the KDE file-picker fix (GH #195), which is the Linux arm of the same function.

entries.title: Typing text at the end of a block whose last line is a SCHEDULED/DEADLINE timestamp keeps the planning date working
entries.notes: Martin's report (no GH issue). The facet layer required the timestamp to occupy the whole source line, so text appended after '>' silently un-badged the deadline while the query layer still saw it. Relaxed to line-leading (OG parity: graph-parser block.cljs:240-244 paragraph-timestamp-block? accepts trailing inlines); the raw is never rewritten. setSchedule now preserves a glued suffix instead of deleting the line. Deliberate divergence kept: mid-text 'Discuss DEADLINE: <...>' stays body content in Tine.

entries.title: Splitting a pane from the journals feed opens the selected day's page plainly, not an unrelated day's bullet zoomed and pinned
entries.notes: Martin's report (no GH issue). A second journals pane is forbidden, so the split substituted the last kind:page route from the tab's back-history verbatim - including its block zoom field - and duplicateActiveSnapshot copied the pinned flag. The substitute is now the day page containing the selected feed block, with block cleared and pinned false (fallbacks: today's day page, then the first visible feed day). Non-journals mirror splits are unchanged. No E2E journey covers Esc -> pane-select -> split from journals.

entries.title: Table columns can be dragged into a new order and the order survives a restart
entries.notes: Table view only (Martin's decision); the grid view is untouched because its tine.col-widths/aggregates are index-keyed and would need remapping. Order persists through the existing tine.fields schema, materialized with declareFreshSchema() on the first drag; formula columns are not serializable in that schema and stay pinned at the end. Necessity gate sits on the schema edge cases (no schema, partial schema, formula boundary, both schema homes, click-still-sorts).

entries.title: On a phone the PDF reader can always be closed: the Close button stays on screen and the Android back gesture (or Escape) closes the reader
entries.notes: Martin's report. The PDF pane was a fixed-width (min 320, default 560px) non-shrinking flex sibling clipped by .content-row overflow:hidden, and Close was the LAST toolbar button, so on a 390px phone it was off-screen while the pane covered everything; the pane was neither a route nor a transient layer, so Android Back walked past it. Now: mobile renders it as a deliberate full-width takeover, Close leads the control cluster, low-priority tools collapse into the existing More menu through a pdftoolbar container query, and the pane registers as a transient layer (inner Find/settings/outline/highlight popovers peel first) so Back and Escape both close it. Chromium proof at 390px with an Android UA: fail-before the Close button was outside the viewport, after it is reachable. Real-device Android verification is NOT done.

entries.title: Typing a multi-character Ctrl+F query does not repeatedly select and replace the text after each debounced search
entries.notes: The focus effect tracked committed query state as well as the explicit focus request, so each debounce reran input.select(). Focus/select-all now keys only on the explicit request: repeat Ctrl+F still selects the query, while ordinary commits preserve the collapsed caret. The existing release E2E now crosses the debounce between characters once instead of relying only on atomic setValue.

entries.title: Selecting a task-state slash command anywhere in a block semantically replaces or adds its leading task marker, matching Logseq
entries.notes: Task rows used to be literal insertions. They now clear the slash trigger and call the shared setMarker primitive like OG, covering every supported marker, plain and already-task blocks, multiline/property content, priority placement, Markdown/Org, one commit, and end-of-block caret placement.

entries.title: Reference-only page names synthesize namespace descendants in the sidebar, namespace macro, and page Hierarchy without appearing in All Pages
entries.notes: The shared page-name inventory unions physical pages with Rust's warmed referenced-name cache case-insensitively; physical display spelling wins. The physical list remains independently keyed to graph/page-inventory revisions, while reference names refresh after dataRev and stale graph responses are discarded.
